title: Consultation: Draft Guidelines for Canadian Drinking Water Quality, Arsenic - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation: Draft Guidelines for Canadian Drinking Water Quality, Arsenic

## Current status: Open
Opened on March 7, 2025, and will close to    new input on May 6, 2025.
Health Canada has drafted guidelines for drinking water quality    related to arsenic . We’re looking for feedback, in particular on the approach used to develop the    guidelines and on the potential costs of implementing them.

## Join in: How to participate
Send an email to water-consultations-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca with your ideas or comments to make yourself heard.

### Participate  by mail
Send a letter with your ideas and  input to the address in contact information below.

## Who is the focus of this consultation
We will engage  with:
- federal,       provincial and territorial regulatory and health protection agencies
- industry       stakeholders
- academic       communities
- non-governmental       organizations

## Key questions for discussion
Your ideas and  inputs are sought on the:
- proposed       approach
- potential       impacts of implementation
We will use the information we gather  from this consultation to revise the guidelines as needed.
Water and Air Quality Bureau Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave West Address Locator 4903B Ottawa ON  K1A 0K9 Email: water-consultations-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca
